steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The notes establish one key difference between the two cartographic traditions: Venetian portolan charts were sparse in illustrations, while Majorcan ones were richly illustrated. The correct choice must accurately state this contrast without reversing or distorting the facts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — reverses the facts: attributes rich illustration to Venetian charts and sparseness to Majorcan ones, which is the opposite of what the notes say. ✗
  • Choice B — fails to explain the difference: describes a feature shared by both traditions rather than the distinction between them. ✗
  • Choice C — misattributes a feature: wrongly assigns a general characteristic to only the Venetian tradition. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Majorcan charts richly illustrated, unlike sparser Venetian ones': states the contrast directly and accurately as the notes describe it. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
